full_text:
Ubuntu Security Notices

USN-8041-1

 

 

 

 

 

 
USN-8041-1: Dottie vulnerability

 

 

 

 
Publication date

 

 

 
12 February 2026

 

 

 

 

 
Overview

 

 

 
Dottie could be made to run programs if it received specially crafted
network traffic.

 

 

 

 

 
Releases

 

 

 

 22.04 LTS

20.04 LTS

 

 

 

 

 

 

 Open side navigation
 

 

 
Packages

 

 node-dottie - Fast and safe nested object access and manipulation in JavaScript
 

 

 
Details

 

 

Yuhan Gao and Peng Zhou discovered that Dottie was vulnerable to prototype
pollution when altering the proto magical attribute. An attacker could
possibly use this issue to achieve remote code execution.

 

 

 

Yuhan Gao and Peng Zhou discovered that Dottie was vulnerable to prototype
pollution when altering the proto magical attribute. An attacker could
possibly use this issue to achieve remote code execution.

 

 

 

 
Update instructions

 

 

 

 

In general, a standard system update will make all the necessary changes.

 Learn more about how to get the fixes.
 

 

 

 

 
The problem can be corrected by updating your system to the following package versions:

 

 

 

 

 Ubuntu Release
 Package Version
 

 22.04

LTS jammy

 node-dottie – 

2.0.2-4ubuntu0.1~esm1

  

Ubuntu Pro
 Fix available with Ubuntu Pro via ESM Apps. A community fix might become publicly available in the future.

 

 

 20.04

LTS focal

 node-dottie – 

2.0.2-1ubuntu0.1~esm1

  

Ubuntu Pro
 Fix available with Ubuntu Pro via ESM Apps. A community fix might become publicly available in the future.

 

 

Reduce your security exposure

 

 Ubuntu Pro provides ten-year security coverage to 25,000+ packages in Main and Universe repositories, and it is free for up to five machines.
 

 

 Get Ubuntu Pro
 

 

References

 

 

 CVE-2023-26132
 

 

 

 CVE-2023-26132
